Petition For Order Determining Entitlement To Distribution Of Estate [Probate Code Section 11700-05]
Off Calendar Jul-12-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Corey Steady for (petitioner & beneficiary) Childhelp, Inc.

The Court stated that there have been no objections. Mr. Steady confirmed that the attorney for the executor has no objections. The Court stated that it has reviewed the petition, that it is an unusual request, that the intent is that the decedent wanted the proceeds from distribution to provide ongoing funds and asked how the purchase of rights provides such. Mr. Steady reported that Childhelp, Inc. owns the copyright, that it has contracts with Netflix and ITunes, that it has been shown at Sundance, that Childhelp is a nonprofit organization to help prevent child abuse, that it can sell the movie to schools and outreach organizations, that it has the ability to earn through distribution of up front purchases. The Court confirmed that the contracts do not inhibit or prohibit the right to sell. Mr. Steady stated that they own the copyright. The Court stated that it will take the Petition for Order Determining Entitlement to Distribution of Estate under submission and that it satisfies the intent and should be granted.

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (71 Rosenkranz St. S.F.)
Off Calendar Nov-06-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David J. Nearon for John Kendall Evans.

The Court inquired whether or not anyone from SFRM was present and whether anyone from SFRM wished to exercise a first refusal. There being no responses, the Court proceeded with the sale. The sale commenced and overbids were presented. At the final overbid, the sale was confirmed at $1,270,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of a new proposed order). An additional bond in the amount of $15,000.00 is required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Aug-26-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Nearon for (Executor) John Evans.

Mr. Nearon stated that notice of the right of first refusal is in the monthly newsletter which was out prior to July 1, 2014, however, there was a mis-print in the publication and an email went out to all members with corrected dates. Mr. Nearon also stated that the real goal is to sell the property and for the listing agent to receive the full commission, referred to language in the will and stated that the agents are reluctant to go full on with marketing of the property because of the potential forfeiture clause. In addition, Mr. Nearon recommended an order from the court stating that the executor may hire a real estate agent to enter into a listing agreement pursuant to probate rules and that language regarding the issue of forfeiture be removed. Mr. Nearon also informed the court that an inventory and appraisal would be submitted fairly soon. The petition for instructions will be deemed under submission on July 31, 2014. Mr. Nearon is to file a declaration which is to include a copy of the newsletter.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy M. Jones. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-30-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Nearon for John Evans.

Mr. Nearon explained that IRB and TLO are search data base companies that are used by Private Investigators and Law Enforcement. Mr. Nearon also stated that the Petitioner has known the Decedent for 30 years, that the Decedent was reclusive, that the Petitioner knew that the Decedent had a deceased twin brother and no parents, and that the Petitioner has no idea of any next of kin. In addition, Mr. Nearon stated that The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Court Reporter: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
